texto: Controlling the position of one's body and keeping a straight back are not contemplation, but can in fact become an obstacle to contemplation. ...when leaving the body 'uncontrolled' is spoken of, what is meant is simply allowing the body to remain in an authentic, uncorrected condition, in which it is not necessary to modify or improve anything. This is because, since all our attempts at correcting the body come from the reasoning mind, they are all false and artificial.
explicacao: Citação de Namkhai Norbu sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Controlling the position of one's body and keeping a straight back are not contemplation, but can…” articula desenvolvimento-pessoal, 1989, asana em registro de desenvolvimento pessoal, com relevância para contextos como asana e dzogchen.

texto: To produce a primary [karmic] cause which is potentially capable of having an effect, three things are necessary: intention, the actual action, and then satisfaction.
explicacao: Citação de Namkhai Norbu sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “To produce a primary [karmic] cause which is potentially capable of having an effect, three thing…” articula reflexao, 1989, buddhism em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como buddhism e karma.

frase_original: To produce a primary [karmic] cause which is potentially capable of having an effect, three things are necessary: intention, the actual action, and then satisfaction.

texto: The light of the sun is the manifestation of the clarity of the sky; and the sky is the basic condition necessary for the manifestation of the sun's light. So, too, in the sky two, three, four, or any number of suns could arise; but the sky always remains indivisibly one sky. Similarly, every individual's state of presence is unique and distinct, but the void nature of the individual is universal, and common to all beings.
explicacao: Citação de Namkhai Norbu sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “The light of the sun is the manifestation of the clarity of the sky; and the sky is the basic con…” articula reflexao, 1989, dzogchen em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como dzogchen e enlightenment.

frase_original: The light of the sun is the manifestation of the clarity of the sky; and the sky is the basic condition necessary for the manifestation of the sun's light. So, too, in the sky two, three, four, or any number of suns could arise; but the sky always remains indivisibly one sky. Similarly, every individual's state of presence is unique and distinct, but the void nature of the individual is universal, and common to all beings.

texto: But karma is not in fact a material accumulation, and does not depend on externals; rather its power to condition us depends on the obstacles that impede our knowledge. If we compare our karma and the ignorance that creates it to a dark room, knowledge of the primordial state would be like a lamp, which, when lit in the room, at once causes the darkness to disappear, enlightening everything. In the same way, if one has the presence of the primordial state, one can overcome all hindrances in an instant.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Namkhai Norbu: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a sabedoria. O trecho “But karma is not in fact a material accumulation, and does not depend on externals; rather its po…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, 1989, dzogchen pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reflexao-noturna.

If we compare our karma and the ignorance that creates it to a dark room, knowledge of the primordial state would be like a lamp, which, when lit in the room, at once causes the darkness to disappear, enlightening everything. In the same way, if one has the presence of the primordial state, one can overcome all hindrances in an instant.
